title: When Love Became Memory
content: There came a day when I realized I had stopped waiting. I no longer expected your name to appear on my phone or imagined accidentally meeting you in familiar places. The change was so gradual that I almost missed it. Love had not disappeared overnight. Instead, it had slowly transformed into something quieter. It became gratitude for the years we shared, lessons carried into new relationships, and a gentle smile whenever an old song reminded me of who we once were. Memory had softened everything that time could not erase. Looking back, I no longer measured our story by the fact that it ended. I measured it by the person it helped me become. Perhaps that is the final shape of lasting love—not possession, not permanence, but the quiet ability to remember someone with affection long after life has carried both hearts in different directions.
